tema: Modals of Obligation — Past (had to / didn't have to / needn't have)

vocabulario.bandera: lo hizo pero no hacía falta
vocabulario.traduccion: no hacía falta que (lo hizo, mas fue innecesario)
no se le permitió / no pudo

banco_oraciones.oracion: Ben had to buy a new phone.
Sofía had to work all weekend to finish the project.
Kenji needn't have brought an umbrella — it didn't rain.
We had to wait two hours at the airport.
Sofía didn't have to pay for the taxi; her client covered it.
We didn't have to wear a uniform at that school.
Ben didn't have to explain twice; everyone understood.
Did you have to work last Saturday?
Did she have to ask permission first?
Why did they have to leave so early?
Did Ben have to buy a new phone? Yes, he did.
Did you have to pay? No, I didn't.
Did Kenji need the umbrella? No, he needn't have brought it.

interaccion: Kenji: I brought an umbrella just in case.
Sofía: You **needn't have** — it hasn't rained here in weeks.
Kenji: Better safe than sorry! Did you **have to bring** anything special for the trip?
Sofía: I **had to bring** my whole design kit. Nothing 'just in case' about that.

banner_diagnostico.opcion_a: I mustn't pay, my client covered it.
banner_diagnostico.opcion_b: I didn't have to pay, my client covered it.

inicio.definicion_pragmatica: In the past, 'had to' shows obligation, 'didn't have to' shows it wasn't necessary, and 'needn't have' shows you did something that turned out to be unnecessary.

filas: It wasn't necessary (and maybe didn't happen)
You did it, but it wasn't necessary
You needn't have brought an umbrella

ejercicios.pregunta: Ben ___ buy a new phone after losing his.
Sofía ___ pay for the taxi, her client covered it.
Kenji ___ brought an umbrella, it didn't rain.

ejercicios.afirmacion: 'Didn't have to' means it was forbidden.
'Needn't have + participle' means you did something unnecessary.

ejercicios.texto: We ___ wait two hours at the airport. (it was necessary)
She ___ ask permission, it was her own decision. (not necessary)
You ___ have worried, everything was fine. (unnecessary, but you did it)

ejercicios.respuesta: Ben had to buy a new phone
Sofía didn't have to pay for the taxi

ejercicios.texto_con_error: I mustn't pay, my client covered it.
ejercicios.respuesta: I didn't have to pay, my client covered it.

ejercicios.oracion_base: It was necessary for Sofía to work all weekend.
ejercicios.respuesta: Sofía had to work all weekend.

ejercicios.oracion_base: It wasn't necessary for us to wear a uniform.
ejercicios.respuesta: We didn't have to wear a uniform.

ejercicios.oracion_base: Kenji brought an umbrella, but it was unnecessary.
ejercicios.respuesta: Kenji needn't have brought an umbrella.

ejercicios.oracion_base: We had to wait two hours.
ejercicios.instruccion: Write the negative form (it wasn't necessary).
ejercicios.respuesta: We didn't have to wait two hours.

ejercicios.pregunta: Did you have to work last Saturday? (short answer, No)

guion: Sofía: I had to redo the whole logo three times for that client.
Kenji: That sounds exhausting. Did you have to start from zero?
Sofía: No, I didn't have to — I just adjusted the colors.
Kenji: I brought my umbrella for nothing today.
Sofía: You needn't have, it's been sunny all week.
Kenji: Better safe than sorry, as always.

comprension.pregunta: Did Sofía have to start the logo from zero?
opciones: No, she just adjusted the colors
comprension.respuesta: No, she just adjusted the colors

comprension.pregunta: Why didn't Kenji need the umbrella?

reading.texto: Sofía spent Saturday explaining her week to Kenji. 'I had to redo the client's logo three times,' she said, 'but I didn't have to start over completely — I just kept adjusting the colors until he was happy.' Kenji admitted he'd brought an umbrella that morning just in case. 'You needn't have,' Sofía laughed, 'it hasn't rained in Medellín for two weeks.' Kenji shrugged: growing up in Osaka, he'd learned that you never really know with weather — better to carry an umbrella you don't need than need one you didn't bring.

preguntas.afirmacion: Sofía had to start the logo completely from zero.
Kenji brought an umbrella that turned out to be unnecessary.
It rained heavily that day in Medellín.
